evidence.description: api.ts has 'use client' directive at top of file
evidence.detail: Line 1 of apps/dashboard/src/lib/api.ts is exactly: "use client"; — This ensures the module is never executed on the server during SSR, preventing refreshPromise from leaking across concurrent SSR requests.

evidence.description: refreshPromise is module-level but guarded by typeof window check
evidence.detail: refreshPromise is declared at module scope (line 12: let refreshPromise: Promise<boolean> | null = null). The 401 handler at line 55 checks 'if (typeof window === "undefined")' and throws immediately for SSR contexts, never reaching the refreshPromise logic. This is a defense-in-depth guard on top of the 'use client' directive.

evidence.description: All files importing api.ts have 'use client' directive
evidence.detail: 6 files import from @/lib/api: hooks/use-auth.ts, app/login/page.tsx, app/register/page.tsx, app/dashboard/products/page.tsx, app/dashboard/products/[id]/page.tsx, app/dashboard/products/new/page.tsx. All 6 have 'use client' as their first line. No Server Components import api.ts.

evidence.description: Dashboard login page loads correctly without SSR errors
evidence.detail: curl http://localhost:3000/login returned HTTP 200 with fully rendered HTML including login form (email/password fields, Sign In button). The page renders correctly in production mode (next build + next start) with no SSR-related errors in the HTML output.

summary: VAL-FE-C8 passes. api.ts uses 'use client' directive ensuring the module (and its refreshPromise) never runs during SSR. Additionally, a typeof window === 'undefined' guard provides defense-in-depth. All 6 consumers of api.ts are client components.
